COMMENT: How are these actually scored from the datasets?

I am undecided what I think about this approach. Because it isn't just the number of people that matters, it's also how well they are protected... and I wonder if both those things are already to some degree accounted for in the 'impact' assessment at the country scale?

I think I remember rightly that the analysis only considers physical hazards (not e.g. covid-19). If so I wonder if you might find some more relevant examples above that relate to weather/climate. Of course mention the interaction of those with other 'outside' factors and that's it's important when assessing impacts - but make clear you don't consider that specifically here. Also - earthquakes are not really in the remit of what were doing in this project, and I don't know that we consider dam failure flooding? I just wonder if we could have more relevant examples

Only if the underlying datasets are reliable - I am really feeling like I need to see a more critical analysis of the potential weaknesses in these to feel confident in the results. For example, I briefly looked up ThinkHazard and they say in their disclaimer there are uncertainties. I don't have time to read through their documentation - I would like a critical summary of it here. Ditto for the other databases. How reliable are they? How does their reliability vary between countries? Do they have weaknesses. Do they agree with each other? etc.
Is EMDAT supporting standardized multi country comparsion? Presumably that relies on the underlying data being consistent across countries - which I assume it isn't.

Which datasets are used for what? Are the classifications clear (e.g. in the Think hazard (which I'm using as an example since I had it open anyway) it is totally unclear to me how the flooding has been divided it seems to have documentation saying pluvial and fluvial are combined, then it breaks up river and urban flooding). Please give a clear indication of what datasets were used for what hazard and any uncertainties/decisions you had to make when classifying.
Please include how you distinguished thunderstorm impacts from flash flood impacts.

From the interactive maps, e.g. dust is only covered in EM-DAT. Compared to river flooding which is based on 6 different datasets. Can we really compare/prioritise one hazard over the other when this is the case? How important is river flooding if you only use EM-DAT?

It's very difficult to read the numbers on the dark colour.
This type of analysis of the uncertainty needs to be strengthened, it needs to be clear where there are big gaps (which countries are these, do you think one available indicator is enough? And then on top of that, how good are those data sets you are using? Are they better in some countries that others?
nd how much is the use of different datasets for different hazard affecting the ranking?

I'm still not sold on this.
